content_type: Educational demonstration - clinical skill (not FLS task)
reason_not_scored: This video is an instructional demonstration of patient positioning techniques for thoracotomy surgery, not an FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) task. FLS tasks are standardized laparoscopic procedural simulations (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, Intracorporeal Suture). This content shows bedside clinical teaching with multiple trainees and an attending surgeon demonstrating manual positioning of a patient on a bed/table — this is not a timed, scored laparoscopic trainer exercise.

demonstrated_techniques: Patient supine to lateral positioning transition
Support of dependent arm and leg during positioning
Proper alignment of torso for thoracic access
Use of pillows and padding for patient safety and exposure
Communication and coordination among multiple handlers
verbal_cues: Instructor directing trainees on hand placement and support points
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding unsupported limb movement that could cause strain
Maintaining spinal alignment during lateral rotation
Ensuring adequate padding between dependent surfaces and patient
teaching_content.educational_focus: Clinical bedside positioning technique for thoracic surgical access

confidence_rationale: Video clearly labeled as 'Sample of Instructional Video: Patient Positioning for Thoracotomy' with copyright by General & Thoracic Surgery Education. Content is educational clinical demonstration, not a standardized FLS simulator task.
